lures.how_to_fish: Flip or pitch into cover, let the craw fall straight on semi-slack line, then hop it once or twice before the next pitch. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Cast and let it fall to bottom, then drag very slowly with the rod held low; shake the tip just enough to quiver the tail without lifting the head. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Tick it along the bottom with short, nervous strips so the claws scratch and flare; keep it close to the substrate and pause after contact with any rock or root. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Let it sink to the strike zone then retrieve with long, slow strips; the rabbit hair undulates seductively between pulls, so don't rush the retrieve. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Twitch it with slack in the line so the bait darts and glides erratically; pause and let it sink a few inches between twitches. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone.

Two or three sharp twitches, then let it suspend perfectly still for a full 3-5 count before the next sequence — the pause is everything. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone.

flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone.

Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone.

Cast across or slightly downstream, let it jig toward you with short strips so the weighted eyes keep the fly tracking hook-point up. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone.

lures.how_to_fish: Reel at a steady medium pace just above cover, letting the trailer kick; vary depth by angling the rod up or down to keep it in the strike zone. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Slow-roll it along the bottom edge of cover at a steady pace, keeping the blades just ticking; bump any piece of structure and let it rise. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Bang it directly into wood, rock, or stumps so the squared bill deflects the bait sideways — reaction strikes happen on that ricochet. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

flies.how_to_fish: Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Strip in long, smooth pulls so the saddle feathers breathe; add a pause every few strips for followers to close the gap. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Cast across or slightly downstream, let it jig toward you with short strips so the weighted eyes keep the fly tracking hook-point up. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

lures.how_to_fish: Slow-roll it along the bottom edge of cover at a steady pace, keeping the blades just ticking; bump any piece of structure and let it rise. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Yo-yo the blade: snap the rod so the head kicks and vibrates, then let it pendulum back with a controlled semi-slack fall. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Yo-yo it through open water: rip up sharply, let it flutter and sink on a semi-slack line, then rip again; vary fall depth by watching the line angle. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

flies.how_to_fish: Cast across or slightly downstream, let it jig toward you with short strips so the weighted eyes keep the fly tracking hook-point up. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

lures.how_to_fish: Slow-roll it along the bottom edge of cover at a steady pace, keeping the blades just ticking; bump any piece of structure and let it rise. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Start on wind-blown edges or the first active cover line and let it cover water.

Reel at a steady medium pace just above cover, letting the trailer kick; vary depth by angling the rod up or down to keep it in the strike zone. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Start on wind-blown edges or the first active cover line and let it cover water.

Sweep the rod tip side to side in short, rhythmic twitches while reeling in just enough slack — the bait should walk in a steady side-to-side waddle. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

flies.how_to_fish: Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Strip with moderate pulls so the articulated body pulses; mix fast strips with full stops where the fly sinks and the sections hinge naturally. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

Strip in long, smooth pulls so the saddle feathers breathe; add a pause every few strips for followers to close the gap. Keep a steady medium cadence. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Make sure fish can track it by keeping it moving on a clean line through the strike zone.

lures.how_to_fish: Flip or pitch into cover, let the craw fall straight on semi-slack line, then hop it once or twice before the next pitch. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

Hold the weight on the bottom and gently shake the rod tip so the worm quivers in place; move it only a foot or two before letting it settle again. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

flies.how_to_fish: Fish it under an indicator or on a slow hand-twist so the fly hovers level and pulses in place rather than diving nose-first. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Let it sink to the strike zone then retrieve with long, slow strips; the rabbit hair undulates seductively between pulls, so don't rush the retrieve. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Cast across or slightly downstream, let it jig toward you with short strips so the weighted eyes keep the fly tracking hook-point up. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

lures.how_to_fish: Keep the bait just off bottom with tiny shakes and short glides so the minnow hovers naturally in the strike zone. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

Hold the weight on the bottom and gently shake the rod tip so the worm quivers in place; move it only a foot or two before letting it settle again. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

Pitch or flip into cover and let it fall straight; after the drop, drag it slowly along the bottom with minimal rod movement before repositioning. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Cast across or slightly downstream, let it jig toward you with short strips so the weighted eyes keep the fly tracking hook-point up. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Let the cone dig and dart on each strip — medium pulls with pauses so the fly hunts just off the rocks or wood. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Pitch or flip into cover and let it fall straight; after the drop, drag it slowly along the bottom with minimal rod movement before repositioning. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Reel at a steady medium pace just above cover, letting the trailer kick; vary depth by angling the rod up or down to keep it in the strike zone. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Cast across or slightly downstream, let it jig toward you with short strips so the weighted eyes keep the fly tracking hook-point up. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it above cover or along the upper edge of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

lures.how_to_fish: Flip or pitch into cover, let the craw fall straight on semi-slack line, then hop it once or twice before the next pitch. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Hold the weight on the bottom and gently shake the rod tip so the worm quivers in place; move it only a foot or two before letting it settle again. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

flies.how_to_fish: Fish it under an indicator or on a slow hand-twist so the fly hovers level and pulses in place rather than diving nose-first. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Let the cone dig and dart on each strip — medium pulls with pauses so the fly hunts just off the rocks or wood. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it in the lower part of the zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

lures.how_to_fish: Crawl it slowly along bottom with short drags and tiny hops; the smaller profile should stay in the fish's face longer than a bulkier jig. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

Hold the weight on the bottom and gently shake the rod tip so the worm quivers in place; move it only a foot or two before letting it settle again. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

Cast and let it fall to bottom, then drag very slowly with the rod held low; shake the tip just enough to quiver the tail without lifting the head. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Let it soak a little longer before each move.

flies.how_to_fish: Fish it under an indicator or on a slow hand-twist so the fly hovers level and pulses in place rather than diving nose-first. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.

Let it sink to the strike zone then retrieve with long, slow strips; the rabbit hair undulates seductively between pulls, so don't rush the retrieve. Slow down and use longer pauses. Keep it around the middle band of the strike zone. Keep the presentation clean and resist overworking it.
